recipe.notes: Tags: Main, Normal, bacon, beef roast, celery, carrots, turnip, guinness, cheese
recipe.title: Guinness beef Stew with Cheddar Herb Dumplings

steps.instruction: Cook the bacon in a large, oven-safe, heavy-based pot or
high-walled saute pan over medium heat.
Remove the bacon, crumble, and set aside, but leave the
bacon fat in the pot. Season the beef with salt and pepper and
fry in the bacon fat until browned on all sides. Remove the
beef from the pan and set aside.
In the same pot, fry the onion, celery, and carrots until soft
and fragrant, adding a little oil if necessary.
Add garlic and fry for another 30 seconds. Stir in the tomato paste.
Pour in the Guinness and Worcestershire sauce. Allow to
come to a simmer and stir with a wooden spoon, scraping up
the browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the beef back to the pot and pour in the chicken broth.
Add the bay leaf and thyme.
Reduce to a simmer and cover. Simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
Add the potatoes or parsnips and the turnip. Simmer for another
½ hour, or until the vegetables are tender.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 2 pounds boneless beef chuck, chopped into

steps.instruction: Remove the bay leaf and thyme branches. If the stew is still
thin, mix a tablespoon of cornstarch with a tablespoon of cold

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 2 pounds boneless beef chuck, chopped into

steps.instruction: water to form a slurry. Mix the slurry into the stew and bring the
mixture to a boil. Reduce to a simmer again, stirring occasionally,
and add in the mushrooms if desired. Cook for 10 minutes, uncovered,

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 2 pounds boneless beef chuck, chopped into

steps.instruction: until the stew thickens and the mushrooms are cooked through.
Stir the bacon back in. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Stir together the self-rising flour and garlic powder in a medium
bowl. Cut in the shortening until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the cheddar cheese, then add the milk and stir until the dry
ingredients are moistened.
Make small balls with the dough and place them on top of the

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 2 pounds boneless beef chuck, chopped into

steps.instruction: stew, leaving them room to expand– they grow a lot as they cook.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 2 pounds boneless beef chuck, chopped into

steps.instruction: Place the stew in the oven uncovered and bake until the dumplings
are browned and cooked through, about 30 to 40 minutes.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 2 pounds boneless beef chuck, chopped into

steps.instruction: Garnish the stew with parsley and serve.
